differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with Lupus Nephritis (Class V/Membranous) and secondary hypercoagulability
Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) with catastrophic APS or renal vein thrombosis
next_steps: Serologic evaluation: ANA, dsDNA, anti-Smith, complement levels (C3/C4), and antiphospholipid panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Perform urgent renal biopsy to confirm histologic pattern of glomerulonephritis
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., heparin drip) given bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary emboli
Obtain formal transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for cardiac source of emboli and pulmonary hypertension
Initiate pulse corticosteroid therapy once infection is ruled out via culture data or if clinical suspicion for autoimmune etiology is high
Monitor renal function, electrolytes, and serial respiratory status
